title: WorldManifest
description: World-level configuration: the canonical entry point and metadata not tied to any single room or item.

schema_version.description: Format version of this world file. The engine refuses worlds newer than it supports. Defaults to 1 when omitted.

start_room_id.description: Room the player spawns in at world init. Must match a RoomTemplate id.

title.description: Display title for the world. Not yet wired to the UI.

layers.description: The layer stack that defines this world's genre. Each entry activates and configures one engine subsystem; the combination is the genre. Omit or leave empty to use built-in engine defaults.

id.description: Which engine subsystem this layer activates (space, combat, dialogue, ...).

mode.description: The variant of that subsystem, when it has more than one (e.g. combat single_exchange vs turn_order).

encounters.description: Initial enemy placements: class_id + room_id pairs spawned at world init.

npc_placements.description: Initial NPC placements: npc_id + room_id pairs placed at world init.
